selectivity_implications.recommendation: MCCB 600A AIC 65 kA — adequate against 22.00 kA at MSP intake (3.0× margin). Verify nameplate marking of series-combination rating if downstream sub-panel breakers are series-rated per NEC 2023 Article 240.86; SCCR marking on MSP enclosure per NEC 2023 §408.36 + Article 110.10.

MCCB 200A AIC 25 kA — adequate against 12.35 kA at TSP-A incoming (2.0× margin). Tenant fit-out devices downstream must carry SCCR ≥ 12.35 kA per NEC 2023 Article 110.9; verify equipment SCCR labels on HVAC condenser units, lighting contactors, and POS rack.

MCCB 200A AIC 25 kA — adequate against 11.04 kA at TSP-B incoming (2.3× margin). Cascade discrimination with upstream MSP MCCB supported by 100 ft × 250 kcmil sub-feeder impedance step (5.84 → 11.42 mΩ) per NEC 2023 Article 240.87 selectivity assessment.

MCCB 100A AIC 22 kA — adequate against 9.34 kA at CA-P incoming (2.4× margin). Common-areas panel feeds parking-lot lighting contactors and signage; verify lighting-control panel SCCR ≥ 9.34 kA per NEC 2023 Article 110.9.

assumptions: PoCo declared available fault current PFC=22.0 kA at MSP intake (208Y/120V TPN) treated as binding boundary per NEC 2023 §110.24(A) (available fault current field-marking) + NEC 2023 Article 110.9 (interrupting rating) — utility-furnished value supersedes any isolated transformer back-calculation.
Transformer X/R = 8 assumed for 300 kVA pad-mount dry-type distribution unit per IEC 60909-1:2008 Table A.1 (typical low-voltage dry-type X/R range 6–10).

Cable impedance per NEC 2023 Chapter 9 Table 9 (AC resistance + reactance, PVC raceway, 75°C operating temperature): 350 kcmil Cu R=0.041 Ω/1000 ft, X=0.037 Ω/1000 ft; 250 kcmil Cu R=0.054 Ω/1000 ft, X=0.038 Ω/1000 ft; 4/0 AWG Cu R=0.063 Ω/1000 ft, X=0.040 Ω/1000 ft. XHHW conductors share the Table 9 column with THWN — same insulation thermal class for the 75°C ampacity column.

Peak factor κ computed per IEC 60909-0:2016 Eq. 29: κ = 1.02 + 0.98·exp(-3/(X/R)) with κ regenerated at each node from the local X/R. κ ≈ 1.69 at TX-1 + MSP (X/R=8), ≈ 1.66 at MSP.BUS (X/R=7.14), ≈ 1.19 at TSP-A (X/R=1.72), ≈ 1.16 at TSP-B (X/R=1.54), ≈ 1.11 at CA-P (X/R=1.26) reflecting the resistive contribution of the tenant sub-feeders.

Motor contribution disregarded: connected motor inventory = 0 kW at the fault-level cascade tier. Retail tenant HVAC fan-coil-unit (FCU) and condenser motors are distributed across tenants but each individual unit is fractional-kW; per IEC 60909-0:2016 §3.8.1 ΣI_rM = 0 A < 1% × 22000 A = 220 A threshold → motor contribution explicitly excluded.

AIC (Ampere Interrupting Capacity) verification per NEC 2023 Article 110.9 — devices must be capable of interrupting available fault current at their terminals. SCCR marking per NEC 2023 §408.36 (panelboards) + Article 110.10 (component protection) verified at MSP enclosure.

Series-combination rating per NEC 2023 Article 240.86 may be applied for downstream breakers if engineering-supervised installation per (A) or manufacturer-tested combination per (B); cascade calculation in this study assumes fully-rated devices throughout (no series ratings claimed).
tool_call_pending: true — calc.iec60909_cascade deterministic execution pending per shared runtime contract.

rationale.chat_summary: US 4-tenant retail strip mall, 208Y/120V TPN. 300 kVA pad-mount TX feeds MSP (600A) then TSP-A (200A, 80 ft), TSP-B (200A, 100 ft), CA-P (100A, 120 ft). PoCo declared 22.0 kA at MSP binds the cascade per NEC 2023 §110.24. Ik"max range 9.34–22.00 kA across LV; HV-1 28.0 kA. Motor load 0 kW — IEC 60909-0:2016 §3.8.1 threshold not met → EXCLUDED. MCCB AIC 65 kA + 25 kA + 22 kA verified per NEC 2023 Article 110.9.

sections.title: Source Specification
sections.summary: Single-source utility supply: 300 kVA PoCo pad-mount transformer fed from 13.2 kV radial primary. No standby generator, no UPS. Treated as far-from-generator per IEC 60909-0:2016 §3.4.

decisions.label: Source modelling — far-from-generator
decisions.summary: Utility transformer with no rotating generation on bus during fault; constant Thévenin source.

sections.title: Cascade Topology
sections.summary: 7-node engineer-declared cascade mirrors the SLD topology (us-strip-mall-msp-tenants): HV-1 → TX-1 → MSP → MSP.BUS → {TSP-A, TSP-B, CA-P}. Three parallel sub-feeders branch from a common MSP busbar serving two retail tenants and a common-areas panel.

decisions.label: Engineer-declared topology
decisions.summary: Author-supplied cascade matching the architectural drawing — single MSP serving three tenant sub-panels with no inter-tenant tie or generator.

sections.title: HV-side Assumptions
sections.summary: 13.2 kV PoCo radial supply, declared PFC 28 kA at primary, X/R = 7 (typical urban distribution per IEC 60909-1:2008 Table A.1). Applying the HV voltage factor c_max = 1.10 per IEC 60909-0:2016 Table 1: ZQ_HV = (1.10 × 13200) / (√3 × 28000) ≈ 0.2994 Ω. Referred to LV via turns ratio (208/13200)² ≈ 2.48 × 10⁻⁴, the HV contribution at LV terminals is ≈74 µΩ — negligible against transformer impedance but retained in the cascade audit.

decisions.label: c_max = 1.10 HV
decisions.summary: Voltage factor selected for Un = 13.2 kV per IEC 60909-0:2016 Table 1.

sections.title: Transformer + Source Impedances
sections.summary: Z_TX nameplate at LV = Z_pu × U²_LV / S = 0.0575 × 208² / 300 000 = 8.29 mΩ (isolated, infinite-source assumption). With X/R = 8 nameplate: R_TX = 8.29 / √65 = 1.03 mΩ, X_TX = 8.22 mΩ. An isolated calculation would yield Ik" = 1.05 × 208 / (√3 × 8.29 mΩ) ≈ 15.2 kA at TX LV terminals against an infinite HV source. However, the PoCo-declared 22.0 kA at MSP exceeds this isolated value — typical of urban 13.2 kV networks where the upstream system impedance is small and the transformer dominates. The cascade is anchored to the PoCo declaration and TX-1 is back-calculated with the X/R = 8 nameplate constraint as ≈23.78 kA accounting for the 10 ft × 350 kcmil Cu service cable to MSP.

decisions.label: PoCo declaration is binding
decisions.summary: NEC 2023 §110.24(A) requires field-marking of available fault current at service equipment; the PoCo-furnished 22.0 kA at MSP is the design-binding value. The isolated TX calculation against an infinite source yields ≈15.2 kA — the higher 22.0 kA declaration reflects a stronger upstream network than nameplate-only modelling captures.
decisions.rule: NEC 2023 §110.24(A) — Available fault current field-marking at service equipment

sections.title: Motor Contribution Assessment
sections.summary: Retail strip mall — no industrial process motors, no lift winders at fault-study scale, no large standalone pumps. HVAC fan-coil-unit and rooftop-condenser motors are distributed across tenants but each unit is fractional-kW; the engineering-honest call at the fault-level cascade tier is to treat total motor load as 0 kW. Per IEC 60909-0:2016 §3.8.1 the threshold check is ΣI_rM ≥ 1% × Ik"_no_motors = 1% × 22000 A = 220 A; with ΣI_rM = 0 A the threshold is not met and motor contribution is explicitly excluded from the cascade.

decisions.label: Motor contribution excluded per §3.8.1
decisions.summary: ΣI_rM = 0 A is below the 220 A threshold. No motor-aggregate entry added to sources[]. Future retrofits (large refrigeration compressors for a restaurant tenant, electric-vehicle charging dispensers) would re-open §3.8.1 and require re-running.

sections.title: Per-node Ifault Computation
sections.summary: Ik"max applied with c=1.05 per Table 1 (LV). Ik"max range across cascade: HV-1 28.00 kA → TX-1 23.78 → MSP 22.00 → MSP.BUS 21.58 → TSP-A 12.35 → TSP-B 11.04 → CA-P 9.34 kA. Sub-feeder impedance per NEC 2023 Chapter 9 Table 9 (AC R + reactance, PVC raceway, 75°C). Peak factor κ from IEC 60909-0:2016 Eq. 29 regenerated at each node from the local X/R.

decisions.label: c_max = 1.05 LV; c_min = 0.95 LV
decisions.summary: Per IEC 60909-0:2016 Table 1 for low-voltage networks ≤ 1 kV.

decisions.label: tool_call_pending across cascade
decisions.summary: Per-node Ik"max/Ik"min/ipk values emitted as engineer estimates; deterministic recompute requires calc.iec60909_cascade tool execution.

sections.title: Selectivity Implications
sections.summary: MSP main MCCB 600A AIC 65 kA verified against 22.00 kA at intake (3.0× margin). Tenant MCCBs 200A AIC 25 kA verified against 12.35 kA (TSP-A, 2.0× margin) and 11.04 kA (TSP-B, 2.3× margin). CA-P MCCB 100A AIC 22 kA verified against 9.34 kA (2.4× margin). All comply with NEC 2023 Article 110.9. SCCR marking on the MSP enclosure per NEC 2023 §408.36 + Article 110.10. Series-combination ratings per NEC 2023 Article 240.86 not claimed — all devices fully rated for the prospective fault at their location.

decisions.label: AIC verification per NEC 2023 Article 110.9
decisions.summary: Device interrupting ratings (AIC) exceed applied prospective fault at each node by at least 2.0×.

decisions.label: SCCR marking per NEC 2023 §408.36 + Article 110.10
decisions.summary: Panelboard SCCR ≥ maximum available fault current at terminals; component protection coordination required.

sections.title: Compliance + Assumptions
sections.summary: Cascade compliant with NEC 2023 + IEC 60909-0:2016. Explicit assumptions: PoCo declared PFC binding per NEC 2023 §110.24(A); transformer X/R = 8 from IEC 60909-1:2008 Table A.1; cable Z from NEC 2023 Chapter 9 Table 9; motor contribution excluded per IEC 60909-0:2016 §3.8.1 strict threshold; AIC verification per NEC 2023 Article 110.9 + §408.36 + Article 110.10 for SCCR; series-combination ratings per Article 240.86 not claimed; calc.iec60909_cascade tool execution pending.

decisions.label: NEC 2023 Article 285 SPD recommendation
decisions.summary: Type 1 SPD recommended at MSP service entrance and Type 2 SPDs at each tenant sub-panel per NEC 2023 Article 285 for transient-overvoltage protection on commercial occupancy with significant point-of-sale and HVAC controls electronics. Retail tenants frequently churn equipment loads — SPDs simplify subsequent fit-outs.
